dialogos.texto_en: Yo, is this where you've been, Itsuki?
Oh, Uesugi-kun?\nWhat brings you here?
Huh?
Ahaha, it looks like we bumped into each other.
Oh, you're here too, Yotsuba.
Yeah, I was just about to ask Itsuki.
Huh? Did both of you come here for the same reason?
Yeah. Itsuki, do you have anything you want to do\nafter this?
Well...didn't you make plans though, Yotsuba?
Yeah, but I thought\nI'd ask everyone what they want to do again, you know?
Sightseeing or shopping or whatever,\nI don't care.
Well, let's see.\nFirst, how about some Okinawan sweets\nthat are only available here?
Sweets!\nOh yeah, the desserts at the hotel restaurant\nwere REALLY delicious.
Yes! They were delectable!
There are many shops that use\nplenty of tropical fruits and specialty \nOkinawan sweet potatoes in their sweets!
With Okinawa, you often hear about brown sugar, don't\nyou? The manufacturing technology for brown sugar was\napparently introduced from China in the early Edo era.
Most of the coloring of brown sugar comes from molasses,\nvitamins, and minerals, doesn't it?
Although it's only in trace amounts,\nsome people may be able to sense differences in flavor.
Oh, wow, that's interesting.
My my, you really know your stuff.\nI can't believe you used to be a straight-F student.
It seems like you're underestimating me, Uesugi-kun.\nEveryone has their own areas of expertise.\nWhen it comes to sweets, I am a master.
...I can't argue with you when it comes to taste.\nI'll back down for today.
You're right, Uesugi-san doesn't stand a chance.
But you're amazing, Itsuki.\nWhen did you have time to study all that?
Well, it's because I have a strong interest in it.\nIt also comes in handy when I write reviews%p-2;――
Ah! No, I mean!\nMy reviews are, well, something I do personally!
(She's still trying to hide it...\n Everyone already knows that the famous reviewer\n 'M·A·Y' is really Itsuki.)
Hmhm, let's start with a tour of Okinawan sweets.\nIs there anything else?
Umm...
That's right.\nI saw someone standing on the ocean earlier.\nIs that also an activity?
Isn't that surfing?
They were standing on something like a board,\nbut it wasn't surfing.\nThey also had something like an oar...
That's probably stand-up paddleboarding.\nIt's a marine activity also known as 'SUP.'
Wow, there's something like that too.
You use a large, specialized board,\nso there are many ways to enjoy it.
You can paddle like you're in a kayak%p-2;――
Or, you can paddle while you're standing up,\nlike a boatman.\nIt feels like you're taking a walk on the sea.
You can also do things\nlike surfing or doing yoga on the board.
Ah! I've seen water yoga in a magazine before.\nSo that's what it was.
Yoga on the water?\nIs that for core strength?
Yes, the instability that the water has is\nimportant for building and maintaining balance\nin the body.
Wow...
Seems like it'd be a challenging activity for me.
Oh come on,\neven you, Uesugi-san, can do it if you practice.
You can sit down if it's feeling dicey,\nand if you do fall, you'll just fall into the water.
That's right!\nRecently, even in tourist areas,\nthere are river tours using 'SUP.'
River tours?\nWow, I've never heard of those before.
Even in familiar towns,\npeople are finding new things\nwhen riding along the river.
Yotsuba, you'd master it quick.\nWith your good motor skills and core strength.
Hehe, thanks but actually,\nI'm already pretty confident in my abilities.
That's my Yotsuba.\nI want to try water yoga too.
How do you feel about surfing, Uesugi-san?
The SUP is bigger than a surfboard,\nso even beginners can do it with ease.
Surfing...
Uesugi-kun, surfing...
W-well, it's all about trying new things, right?\nYou might find a hidden talent, Uesugi-san!
Let's put surfing aside\nand just take a walk on the water.
I agree!\nRecently, even in tourist areas,\nthere are river tours using 'SUP.'
River tours?\nWow, I've never heard of those before.
Even in familiar towns,\npeople are finding new things\nwhen riding along the river.
I'd like to take a walk.\nSeems like it'd feel really nice.
Moreover, this beautiful ocean, you know.\nIt's definitely refreshing.
If possible, I'd like to try water yoga.
Maybe with a little practice,\nyou'll be able to pick it up quick.
Really? But, what if I fail...
If it's your first time, it's not weird to fail.\nActually, you should try it keeping in mind\nthat it's natural to fail.
Plus, since we're wearing swimsuits,\nit's okay to fall!
That's true.\nIf I'm afraid of failing from the beginning,\nI won't be able to do anything at all.
That's right!\nRecently, even in tourist areas,\nthere are river tours using 'SUP.'
River tours?\nWow, I've never heard of those before.
Even in familiar towns,\npeople are finding new things\nwhen riding along the river.
New discoveries...\nThat might be fun.
For now, we can consider it as practice\nfor getting used to 'SUP.'\nThat might be a good candidate, don't you think?
That's right.\nWe haven't heard everyone's thoughts yet.
In that case, I'll resume the survey.\nSee you, Itsuki.
Right, see you later.
